finding: Frequency and timing agreement can still be produced by an environmental tone. A per-run challenge that cryptographically randomizes both frequency and active time slot, while treating every other slot as a negative control, materially reduces that false-positive path.

primary_sources.used_for: Oscillator scheduling and browser audio timing semantics

primary_sources.status: W3C First Public Working Draft published 2025-04-22; work in progress, not an endorsed Recommendation
primary_sources.used_for: Crypto.getRandomValues interface and explicit source-status boundary

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit source

evidence_strength: deterministic source behavior; no browser microphone or device runtime proof
uncertainty: The gate lowers one false-positive mechanism but cannot exclude a compromised random source, an adversarial sound source that observes the challenge, microphone processing artifacts, or a user not hearing the tone.
falsification_route: Replay the selected frequency in any declared control slot or produce repeated matching detections. The classifier must change from challenge_consistent to ambiguous_environmental_match.
privacy_boundary: Persist only challenge nonce, selected frequency, timing windows, and derived detection intervals. Do not retain microphone audio unless separately consented and governed.
